posts.text:
Just a heads up that young people won't engage with your scientific research or activism campaigns if you use generative AI images and videos.

They probably won't trust you or your organization moving forward ether, so hire a creative like the good ol' days or don't use any graphics at all.

posts.text:
The additional cost of ONE fossil fuel price spike on the scale of 2022 = the ENTIRE COST of Net Zero by 2050. We get precisely nothing in return for the first cost, and a whole new, more secure and cheaper energy system from the second one. 
#NoBrainer
www.theccc.org.uk/2026/03/11/c...

posts.text:
It's been interesting to watch major media outlets put their climate reporting behind a paywall while other articles are not.

I understand the financial reasoning but worry about its overall impact. Climate hushing is bad enough as is; this is only going to make it worse.
posts.quote:

posts.text:
It’s almost like collaborating peacefully to build clean energy projects (and not fighting wars over fossil fuels) is the future.

Read more: goodclimatenews.substack.com/p/edition-38...

posts.text:
Reverend Jesse Jackson called on each of us to be heralds of change, to be messengers of hope; to step forward and say “Send me” wherever we have a chance to make an impact.

How fortunate we were that Jesse Jackson answered that call. What a great debt we owe to him.
posts.quote:
Reverend Jesse Jackson called on each of us to be heralds of change, to be messengers of hope; to step forward and say “Send me” wherever we have a chance to make an impact.

posts.text:
Beware of bad actor messaging campaigns aimed to get you to not take action. They are popping up on my feed here more often.

One just said that “voting truly is the opiate of the masses,” as if voting isn’t the bare minimum way we should engage in our democracy.

Please keep voting 🙏

Please keep voting 🙏

posts.text: Yeah, but for a climate journalist to write an op-ed without talking to activists and then claim we aren’t doing anything is just straight up unprofessional.. even in times of fascism..

posts.text:
This is a marvelous change not only for the Humpback whale 🐋 population but for all of us. We need to keep our gentle giants from going extinct.
Fifty years after New Zealand stopped whaling, humpback population showing signs of recovery | RNZ News share.google/X1HB3JFgaePK...

posts.text: Well, this was insulting to read as a scientist and activist who has been working overtime for the past year to reduce the damage the Trump administration is doing.

posts.text: What I don't understand about climate doomers is how they refuse to accept the fact that every single fraction of a degree of warming we can prevent, whether that be now or in the future, will save lives and ecosystems. Also, adaptation exists, so there's no excuse to give up on activism.
posts.quote: What I don't understand about climate doomers is how they refuse to accept the fact that every single fraction of a degree of warming we can prevent, whether that be now or in the future, will save lives and ecosystems. Also, adaptation exists, so there's no excuse to give up on activism.

posts.text:
Y'all, I am once again in awe of organizing efforts in Eastern Kentucky because I advocated for a Northeast Tennessee-wide sustainability plan when I worked for the government, and not even the most progressive communities in the region were interested.

Here's to more Appalachian climate action!!

posts.text: If you believe people and ecosystems don't deserve help recovering from climate disasters because of who governs the area, then you don't actually care about addressing the climate crisis. You have a weird punishment kink, and you need to be called out for it because your behavior is harmful.

posts.text: Edition 38: March 11, 2026. The Trump administration might be doing everything they can to increase fossil fuel production, but the rest of the world is still committed to expanding clean energy capacity.
posts.quote: Edition 38: March 11, 2026. The Trump administration might be doing everything they can to increase fossil fuel production, but the rest of the world is still committed to expanding clean energy capacity.

posts.text: Edition 37: February 20, 2026. From banks refusing to financial a controversial fossil fuel project to groups suing the EPA over revoking the endangerment finding, our climate activisim is working!
posts.quote: Edition 37: February 20, 2026. From banks refusing to financial a controversial fossil fuel project to groups suing the EPA over revoking the endangerment finding, our climate activisim is working!

posts.text: Edition 35: February 6, 2026. Between Europe hitting a positive climate tipping point and the Trump administration failing yet again to stop offshore wind, it’s nice to be reminded that not all hope is lost.
posts.quote: Edition 35: February 6, 2026. Between Europe hitting a positive climate tipping point and the Trump administration failing yet again to stop offshore wind, it’s nice to be reminded that not all hope is lost.

posts.text: Edition 34: January 23, 2026. Coal Generation Declines in China and India for The First Time in Over 50 Years

posts.text: Edition 31: December 5, 2025. From floods to fires to big polluters taking over negotiations, COP30 felt like a climate disaster fever dream, but that doesn't mean there wasn't any progress.
posts.quote: Edition 31: December 5, 2025. From floods to fires to big polluters taking over negotiations, COP30 felt like a climate disaster fever dream, but that doesn't mean there wasn't any progress.

posts.text: Edition 30: November 15, 2025. From a global treaty on overfishing going into effect to Colombia buying solar panels for low income households, there is still plenty of hope when it comes to the climate crisis.
posts.quote: Edition 30: November 15, 2025. From a global treaty on overfishing going into effect to Colombia buying solar panels for low income households, there is still plenty of hope when it comes to the climate crisis.

posts.text: Edition 29: November 7, 2025. The time for big polluters to be actually held accountable is so close I can taste it!
posts.quote: Edition 29: November 7, 2025. The time for big polluters to be actually held accountable is so close I can taste it!

posts.text: Edition 26: October 10, 2025. From hitting historic clean energy milestones to protecting climate-resilient coral reefs, there is still plenty of good climate news out there.
